arts.ttl: Why Arsenal look like a Mourinho team - but must change to win like one
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta's Arsenal have been likened to a Jose Mourinho team this season - but how accurate are those comparisons and will it bring success?

arts.ttl: ‘No chance’: Sky journalist says Arsenal would now have to pay £200m to sign striker target
arts.sum: Arsenal are finding it difficult to land their top transfer targets at the moment. Arsenal made a bid for Dusan Vlahovic earlier this week, but...

arts.ttl: Aston Villa appear ‘out of nowhere’ to steal signing – Turned up ‘when nobody expected’, paid transfer clause
arts.sum: Aston Villa ‘appeared when nobody expected’ to sign Levante youngster Andrés García, with the Portuguese side now hoping he turns them down. That’s according to journalist Sebastião Sousa Pinto, who provides the Sporting Club de Portugal stance on proceedings as Aston Villa finalise a deal for the right-back. Reports throughout today have stated Aston Villa The post Aston Villa appear ‘out of nowhere’ to steal signing – Turned up ‘when nobody expected’, paid transfer clause appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Arsenal's Slegers 'very happy' with full-time job
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Renee Slegers has said she is "very happy" after being appointed as permanent head coach and revealed there were several moments in recent weeks where she felt that she would really like to lead the north London club.

arts.ttl: Arsenal vs. Aston Villa: How Ollie Watkins can pass Gunners transfer audition after 'scarring' Mikel Arteta
arts.sum: Aston Villa striker Ollie Watkins would be 'absolutely perfect' for Arsenal ahead of Saturday's Premier League showdown, Charles Watts tells Sports Mole.

arts.ttl: January loan, £65m deal, two alternatives - Benjamin Sesko to Arsenal transfer verdict delivered - Matthew Abbott - football.london
arts.sum: So, we will try, and let's see what we can get." Sesko is among four elite strikers linked to Arsenal with Alexander Isak, Viktor Gyokeres and Dusan Vlahovic as the others. "Yes, for sure, because we lost two big players - Bukayo could be out for three months, and Gabby is going to be out for a long time too," he told TNT Sport when asked about signing a new forward.

But buoyed by a derby day victory and the fact that no top-flight team has amassed more home points than us this term, Mikel Arteta will hope to be the Spanish boss celebrating come full-time as we aim to hit new levels under his watch, despite a relentless period of fixtures that will see both teams taste Champions League football again next week. After beating Tottenham Hotspur in midweek to extend our unbeaten run at our house to 13 league games, we face an Aston Villa side on Saturday (5.30pm UK) who were the last team to leave N5 with three Premier League points.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta's job security, how striker signing impacts Arsenal boss, two-word instruction
arts.sum: Arsenal have been linked with a host of top forwards in the January transfer window, but have so far been unable to get a deal over the line as Mikel Arteta looks for reinforcements

arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer news: Mikel Arteta 'gives thumbs-up' to Gunners move for £83m Sporting Lisbon star
arts.sum: Arsenal head coach Mikel Arteta is reportedly in approval of the Gunners' impending swoop for Sporting Lisbon and Sweden striker Viktor Gyokeres.

arts.ttl: Arsenal, Man City dump Man United out of race for next Martin Odegaard?
arts.sum: Manchester United are reportedly out of the race to sign Rosenborg prodigy Sverre Nypan, whose representatives are discussing moves with Manchester City and Arsenal.
